name: Ed Garland
abstract: Edward Bertram Garland (January 9, 1895 – January 22, 1980) was an American jazz double bassist. He was commonly known as Ed Garland, and sometimes Montudie Garland (a nickname he disliked).

concerts.label: Kid Ory and His Creole Jazz Band

works.t: Bill Bailey, Won't You Please Come Home?
I'm Busy and You Can't Come In

about_quotes.speaker: Buddy Burns and Frank Pasley
about_quotes.evidence: Ed Garland on the bass, Minor Hall on the drums, Ory, and myself. I played up there with them for a long time.
about_quotes.doc_title: Buddy Burns and Frank Pasley Interview, 1968-09-23

about_quotes.speaker: Don Ewell
about_quotes.evidence: Oh, yeah, Ed Garland. Yes. Oh, I had a lot of enjoyment playing with that section.

about_quotes.speaker: Manuel "Fess" Manetta and Edward "Kid" Ory
about_quotes.evidence: Ed Garland is only mentioned by the Interviewer in the context of having died recently; the Interviewee does not mention Ed Garland or describe any personal interaction with him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Manuel "Fess" Manetta and Edward "Kid" Ory Interview, 1958-08-26

about_quotes.speaker: Johnny St. Cyr
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Because I was asking for instance, Ed Garland... And I asked him if he was conscious of the chords... [INTERVIEWEE] Well, I, there's lots of guys can do that.

about_quotes.speaker: Robert Lewis
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] I think that fight between Ed Garland and Ed and Ori, Kid Ori, out in San Francisco... [INTERVIEWEE] Well, it's a very expressive form of music.

about_quotes.speaker: Edmond "Doc" Souchon
about_quotes.evidence: It was either of those two were always in demand. Ed Garland. Ed Garland. Garland, but there was still another one. I don't remember.
